title: Step 104: Centroid Accumulation -- averaging destroys geometry

content.purpose: Test centroid accumulation: single d x C matrix replacing the full codebook.
content.method: Maintain a running class centroid matrix. Classify by nearest centroid.
content.result: KILLED. Single d x C matrix: 30% AA. Sparse storage is load-bearing -- averaging destroys task-specific geometry.
content.key_insight: The codebook stores distributed, task-specific prototype geometry. Compressing to a single centroid per class loses spatial structure.
